title: Disable and Enable Existing Shared Users
story: As a product administrator, I want to disable and re-enable shared users, so that I can manage my available license seats without permanently removing users from my account.
description:
Add enable/disable functionality for shared team users. Instead of permanently detaching users (deleting the intermediate record), the system now toggles a status field. Disabled users can still authenticate via Salesforce and see the shared account in their dropdown, but selecting it shows a message that their access has been revoked.

Admins cannot enable more users than their available seat limit. Disabling a user frees up a seat for new invitations.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can disable a shared user from the account management page
Admin can re-enable a previously disabled user
Disabled users still see the shared account in their dropdown
Selecting a disabled account shows a 'contact your administrator' message
Disabled users can still authenticate via Salesforce and access their own account
Admin cannot enable more users than the available seat limit
ux_changes: Enable/disable toggle on the account management page for each shared user. Disabled users see a message when trying to access the revoked account. Account dropdown still shows the shared account.
middleware_changes: Team account attachment uses soft disable (status field) instead of hard detach. Middleware checks account status before granting access.
data_model_changes: Status field added to the team-user intermediate table to track enabled/disabled state.
implementation_notes: Changed from hard detach (deleting the intermediate record) to soft disable (toggling a status field). This preserves the relationship and allows re-enabling without re-inviting.
comment_summary: Developer implemented soft disable with status field, showing disabled message when users try to access a revoked account. Middleware updated to check status before granting access.
